description: The PKM is a soviet/russian belt-fed, general-purpose machine gun. It's chambered in the ancient 7.62x54mmR cartridge, which is heavy and has a lot of kick, but each bullet will go through several zombies, and they come in 250-round belts.

In a controversial move, a toy company released this real-looking replica of the Bolter from Warhammer 40k as a cosplay prop. It's much smaller than the lore dictates, but still very thick and heavy. It became wildly popular a few year before the cataclysm. It fires large nerf darts, which 'happen' to be exactly 40x46mm in size, and the internals 'happen' to be able to trigger and handle the explosion of low velocity grenades. They still managed to sell it on the civilian market, their lawyers arguing civilians wouldn't be able to get their hands on actual grenades anyway. Perhaps you will. It's still toy-grade quality, so don't expect precision or durability.

Private arsenal Nariman noticed the success of the not-40mm bolter toy, and developed an actual militarised version, designed to use high-speed 40x53mm greanades. It's slightly heavier, but military-grade internals ensure good reliability and precision. To the amusement of the general public, the M3A9 ended up being adopted by the US military and issued to heavy infantry grenadiers.
